message:
fix(config,installer): update MCP port to 8876, add startup_timeout_sec, and fix installer awk/sqlite detection

MCP endpoint port migration:
- Update cursor.mcp.json and gemini.mcp.json to use port 8876 instead
  of 8765, matching the current runtime default.

Installer startup timeout support (install.sh):
- Add `startup_timeout_sec = 30` to all TOML config generation paths
  in `setup_single_toml_config()`: initial file creation, in-place
  awk update (with `saw_startup_timeout_in_section` tracking), and
  append-new-section fallback. This gives MCP clients (especially
  Codex) enough time to complete the initial handshake.

Installer awk portability fix (install.sh):
- Replace `BEGIN { IGNORECASE = 1 }` in `extract_migration_error_line()`
  with explicit `tolower($0)` lowering, since IGNORECASE is a gawk
  extension not available on mawk or other awk implementations common
  on minimal Linux installs and macOS. The pattern matching now uses
  the lowercased line variable consistently.

Installer SQLite timestamp detection fix (install.sh):
- Change `sqlite_text_timestamp_columns_remaining()` from
  `SELECT typeof(col) FROM table WHERE col IS NOT NULL LIMIT 1`
  (which reports the storage class of the first non-null row, which
  may be integer even if later rows contain text) to
  `SELECT 1 FROM table WHERE typeof(col) = 'text' LIMIT 1` which
  directly checks whether any text-typed values exist. This correctly
  detects remaining text timestamps in mixed-type columns.

scripts/am startup timeout support:
- Add `startup_timeout_sec = 30` to `sync_toml_mcp_url()` in all
  TOML generation paths: initial file creation, in-place awk update
  (with `saw_startup_timeout_in_section` tracking), section-end flush,
  and append-new-section fallback. Mirrors the install.sh changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
